Public reproduction API surface for notebook and scripting workflows.

Purpose:
    Expose a stable, minimal set of types used to construct, execute, and summarize reproducible evaluation runs from notebooks or scripts. This module serves as the canonical import location for reproduction workflows and hides the internal package layout of the reproduction subsystem.

Owns:
    - The public export surface for reproduction workflows - The stable set of types expected by notebooks and user scripts - The compatibility boundary between user code and internal reproduction implementation modules

Does not own:
    - Dataset loading or caching behavior (implemented in repro_api) - Evaluation execution or orchestration logic (implemented in runner) - Evaluation result computation or report construction (implemented in core.evaluation) - Runtime telemetry or experiment scheduling concerns

Key relationships:
    - Re-exports dataset abstractions from ``answer_engineering.repro_api`` - Re-exports evaluation result structures from ``ae_paper_reproduction.core.evaluation`` - Re-exports progress and reporting utilities from ``ae_paper_reproduction.runner`` - Imported directly by notebooks and reproducibility scripts

Current architecture notes:
    This module is intentionally a façade-only surface.  It does not define behavior and should remain free of orchestration or domain logic. All exported names are explicitly enumerated via ``__all__`` to ensure a deterministic and inspectable public API.

Invariants:
    - Only stable, user-facing reproduction types are exported here - All exports are explicitly listed in ``__all__`` - No runtime orchestration or evaluation logic is implemented in this module

TODO:
    Keep this module small and stable as the reproduction subsystem evolves. If new user-facing workflow primitives are introduced, export them here only after their ownership boundary is clearly established.
